_readme: Declarative constraint registry for the DE pricing optimizer (compiled by scripts/pricing/constraints_lib.py).
Every family ships enabled:false, so the production engine behaves EXACTLY as the validated champion until a family is deliberately switched on.
To activate: set enabled:true, then pass this file's contents as config['constraints'] to de_optimizer.build_problem / optimize (e.g. CONFIG['constraints'] = constraints_lib.load_config(path)).
Validate after editing: python -X utf8 scripts/pricing/constraints_lib.py --config DISCOUNT_PLAN/pricing/pricing_constraints.json
Onboarding another brand = copy this file and edit values; zero code changes.

kpi_bounds._doc: Paper Eq.33: min_frac*K0 <= KPI(x) <= max_frac*K0 vs the baseline (disc0) value K0. null = that side unbounded. KPIs: revenue, volume, nrw, share, spend, profit, margin. Note the champion already hard-wires a revenue floor at 0.98 — the revenue entry here is an EXTRA declarative layer, not a replacement.

pricing_line._doc: Paper Eq.36: within each base_product x city pack family, every member's absolute shelf-price move (p_new - p0, rupees) must be equal within tol_rupees — uniform family price moves, no pack drifting alone.

portfolio_avg_band._doc: Paper Eq.37 (plain-average version): 100*sum(p_new-p0)/sum(p0) must stay within [theta_lo_pct, theta_hi_pct] percent per weekly cycle. Discount cuts RAISE shelf price, so theta_hi caps how fast the portfolio average price may rise.

vw_avg_band._doc: Paper Eq.38-39: volume-weighted average price change band with ENDOGENOUS weights — the new average is weighted by the OPTIMIZED volumes from demand_model at the candidate discounts (not baseline volumes), so the weights move with the decision. Enforced as a soft penalty inside the DE metaheuristic, per the paper's rationale.
